input_row.title: Ensure That Microsoft Defender for App Services Is Set To 'On'
input_row.description: Turning on Microsoft Defender for App Service enables threat detection for App Service, providing threat intelligence, anomaly detection, and behavior analytics in the Microsoft Defender for Cloud.
input_row.rationale: Enabling Microsoft Defender for App Service allows for greater defense-in-depth, with threat detection provided by the Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC). Impact: Turning on Microsoft Defender for App Service incurs an additional cost per resource.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Defender for Cloud 2. Under Management, select Environment Settings 3. Click on the subscription name 4. Select Defender plans 5. Ensure Status is On for App Service Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command: az security pricing show -n AppServices Ensure -PricingTier is set to Standard Audit from PowerShell Run the following command: Get-AzSecurityPricing -Name 'AppServices' |Select-Object Name,PricingTier Ensure the -PricingTier is set to Standard  Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 2913021d-f2fd-4f3d-b958-22354e2bdbcb - Name: 'Azure Defender for App Service should be enabled'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Defender for Cloud 2. Under Management, select Environment Settings 3. Click on the subscription name 4. Select Defender plans 5. Set App Service Status to On 6. Select Save Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command: az security pricing create -n Appservices --tier 'standard' Remediate from PowerShell Run the following command: Set-AzSecurityPricing -Name "AppServices" -PricingTier "Standard" Default Value: By default, Microsoft Defender plan is off. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-detection- capabilities 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/securitycenter/pricings/list 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/securitycenter/pricings/update 4. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.security/get- azsecuritypricing 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-1-enable-threat-detection-capabilities
input_row.impact: Turning on Microsoft Defender for App Service incurs an additional cost per resource.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
The audit can be fully verified via Azure CLI and PowerShell commands as specified in the audit field. The associated commands check the properties of Microsoft Defender for App Services, which is queryable through Azure APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_appservice_defender_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

- **Azure CLI Commands:**
  - Use `az security pricing show -n AppServices` to retrieve the pricing tier for App Services.
  - Expected output for `-PricingTier` should be `Standard` to confirm that Microsoft Defender for App Service is enabled.

- **PowerShell Command:**
  - Use `Get-AzSecurityPricing -Name 'AppServices' | Select-Object Name, PricingTier` to validate similar information through PowerShell.
  - Again, the expected value for `PricingTier` should be `Standard`.

- **Resources to Enumerate:**
  - This program would enumerate across all subscriptions the Azure account has access to, specifically focusing on App Services within those subscriptions.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - If the `PricingTier` is set to `Standard`, the check should pass, indicating that Microsoft Defender for App Services is enabled.
  - If the `PricingTier` is set to anything other than `Standard`, the check should fail.

- **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command:**
  - Command: `az security pricing show -n AppServices` 
  - Expected response should indicate `Standard` for `PricingTier`.
